courses.original_course_name: AHA - BLS Provider - In-person Initial
courses.clean_title: AHA BLS Provider – In-Person
courses.lander_title: BLS Certification for Healthcare Providers (AHA BLS Provider)
courses.original_html:
AHA Basic Life Support (BLS) Provider – In-Person

This American Heart Association BLS Provider course is designed for healthcare professionals such as nurses, EMTs, paramedics, medical students, and other clinical staff who need initial BLS certification or whose certification has expired.

The class includes instructor-led training and hands-on practice covering adult, child, and infant CPR, AED use, and team-based resuscitation skills following current American Heart Association guidelines.

Course length: approximately 3–4 hours

Certification: American Heart Association BLS Provider eCard issued upon successful completion. Valid for two years.

AHA® is a registered trademark of the American Heart Association. 910CPR is an independent training provider aligned with an AHA Training Center.
courses.lander_html:
BLS Certification for Healthcare Providers

The American Heart Association Basic Life Support (BLS) Provider course is designed for healthcare professionals who need CPR certification for clinical work.

This instructor-led class teaches high-quality CPR, AED use, and team-based resuscitation skills used in hospitals, clinics, and emergency medical settings.

Who This Course Is For

  • Nurses and nursing students
  • EMTs and paramedics
  • Medical and dental professionals
  • Respiratory therapists
  • Physical therapists and medical staff
  • Healthcare students and clinical trainees

Course Topics

  • Adult, child, and infant CPR
  • Automated External Defibrillator (AED) use
  • Team-based resuscitation and communication
  • Airway management and ventilation techniques
  • Recognition of cardiac arrest and respiratory emergencies

Course Format

  • Instructor-led classroom training
  • Hands-on skills practice
  • Skills testing using AHA guidelines

Classes typically take 3–4 hours to complete depending on class size.

Certification

Students who successfully complete the course receive a 2-year American Heart Association BLS Provider eCard.

This certification is accepted by hospitals, healthcare employers, and medical training programs nationwide.
courses.seo_description: AHA BLS certification for healthcare providers. Instructor-led CPR training covering adult, child, and infant CPR, AED use, and team-based resuscitation. Earn your 2-year American Heart Association BLS Provider eCard.

courses.original_course_name: AHA - BLS Provider - In-person Renewal
courses.clean_title: AHA BLS Provider – In-Person Renewal
courses.lander_title: AHA BLS Renewal for Healthcare Providers
courses.original_html:
AHA BLS Provider – In-Person – Renewal

This American Heart Association (AHA) Basic Life Support (BLS) Renewal course is designed for healthcare professionals with a current, valid AHA BLS Provider card who are seeking recertification. This course provides a focused review of core CPR and AED skills and allows participants to demonstrate continued competency in high-quality CPR.

Requirement: You must bring a current, valid AHA BLS Provider card to class. If your card is expired, please contact us prior to registration to verify eligibility.

 

This instructor-led, classroom-based course includes video instruction, skills demonstration, and scenario-based practice. Instructor-to-student ratio is maintained at 1:6 during hands-on sessions to meet AHA standards.

We recommend reviewing the BLS Quick Reference Guide prior to class.

Course Highlights:

  • High-quality CPR for adults, children, and infants
  • Use of AED and relief of choking
  • Effective team dynamics and communication
  • Opioid emergencies and maternity considerations
  • Hands-on practice with BVM, pocket mask, and two-rescuer CPR

Course Length:

Approximately 3 hours (minimum per AHA PAM for BLS Renewal)

Certification:

American Heart Association BLS Provider eCard (valid for 2 years), issued upon successful completion of all course components.
courses.lander_html:
AHA BLS Renewal for Healthcare Providers

The American Heart Association BLS Renewal course is designed for healthcare professionals who already hold a current or recently expired BLS Provider certification.

This instructor-led class reviews essential life-saving skills including high-quality CPR, AED use, airway management, and team-based resuscitation.

Who This Course Is For

  • Nurses
  • Physicians
  • EMTs and paramedics
  • Dental professionals
  • Healthcare students

What You Will Learn

  • Adult, child, and infant CPR
  • AED operation
  • Airway management and ventilation
  • Team-based resuscitation
  • Opioid emergency response

Course Format

Instructor-led classroom training with hands-on practice using professional manikins and equipment.

Certification

Students who successfully complete the course receive an American Heart Association BLS Provider eCard, valid for 2 years.

Course Length

Approximately 3 hours.
courses.seo_description: Refresh your CPR skills fast with our AHA BLS Renewal course. Hands-on training for healthcare providers covering high-quality CPR, AED use, airway management, and team-based resuscitation. Earn your AHA BLS Provider eCard.

courses.original_course_name: AHA - BLS Provider - Online Class with in-Person Skill Session - Initial or Renewal
courses.clean_title: AHA BLS Provider – HeartCode + Skills Session
courses.lander_title: AHA HeartCode BLS with In-Person Skills Session
courses.original_html:
AHA BLS Provider – Heartcode (Blended Learning)

The American Heart Association (AHA) Heartcode BLS course uses a blended learning format that includes an interactive online course followed by an in-person skills session. This format is ideal for healthcare professionals who need flexibility without compromising the required hands-on components of CPR training.

Participants complete the online Heartcode BLS Provider course at their own pace and then attend a scheduled in-person skills session with an AHA Instructor. This allows for independent review followed by personalized feedback and evaluation of CPR skills.

Course Highlights:

  • Complete the Heartcode BLS online course independently
  • Attend a 30–60 minute in-person skills session
  • Receive instructor feedback and hands-on practice
  • Demonstrate proficiency in high-quality CPR and AED use
  • Earn a two-year AHA BLS Provider eCard upon successful completion
courses.lander_html:
AHA HeartCode BLS – Online Course + Skills Session

The American Heart Association HeartCode BLS course is a blended learning option for healthcare professionals who need the flexibility of online coursework without skipping the required hands-on skills check.

This format is appropriate for initial certification or renewal when AHA HeartCode BLS is accepted by your employer, school, or licensing body.

How the Course Works

  • Complete the online HeartCode BLS course
  • Attend a short in-person skills session
  • Receive your AHA BLS Provider eCard after successful completion

Who This Course Is For

  • Nurses and nursing students
  • Medical assistants
  • Dental professionals
  • EMTs and paramedics
  • Allied health students
  • Busy healthcare providers who need a flexible certification option

Course Format

This is a blended learning course with a self-paced online module and a short in-person skills session, typically 30–60 minutes.

Certification

Students who successfully complete both parts receive an American Heart Association BLS Provider eCard, valid for 2 years.
courses.seo_description: Complete AHA HeartCode BLS online at your own pace, then finish with a short in-person skills session. Ideal for healthcare professionals needing initial certification or renewal. Earn your AHA BLS Provider eCard after successful completion.

courses.original_course_name: AHA - ACLS Provider - In-person Initial
courses.clean_title: AHA ACLS Provider – In-Person Initial
courses.lander_title: AHA ACLS Certification – In-Person Initial
courses.original_html:
AHA - ACLS Provider – In-person

The Advanced Cardiac Life Support (ACLS) course is designed for healthcare professionals involved in the management of cardiopulmonary emergencies and resuscitation. This in-person, instructor-led training follows American Heart Association guidelines and emphasizes high-performance team dynamics and clinical decision-making in critical situations.

This course is intended for personnel in emergency response, emergency medicine, intensive care, and critical care units. You may add an optional BLS renewal during registration if both certifications are needed.

Course Topics Include:

  • Early recognition and management of respiratory and cardiac arrest
  • Airway management and related pharmacology
  • Post-cardiac arrest care and team communication
  • Identification and treatment of acute coronary syndromes and stroke
courses.lander_html:
AHA ACLS Provider Certification – In-Person

The American Heart Association Advanced Cardiac Life Support (ACLS) Provider course prepares healthcare professionals to manage cardiopulmonary emergencies using evidence-based resuscitation protocols and high-performance team dynamics.

This instructor-led classroom course follows the current American Heart Association guidelines and focuses on rapid recognition of life-threatening conditions, effective airway management, pharmacology, and coordinated team-based resuscitation.

Who This Course Is For

  • Physicians
  • Nurses
  • Paramedics and advanced EMTs
  • Respiratory therapists
  • Critical care and emergency department staff
  • Healthcare providers who respond to cardiovascular emergencies

What You Will Learn

  • Recognition and management of cardiac arrest
  • Airway management and ventilation strategies
  • ACLS pharmacology and medication administration
  • Management of acute coronary syndromes and stroke
  • Post-cardiac arrest care
  • High-performance resuscitation team communication

Course Length

Approximately 8 hours depending on class size and experience level.

Certification

Students who successfully complete the course receive an American Heart Association ACLS Provider eCard valid for 2 years.
courses.seo_description: Get AHA ACLS certified with hands-on instructor-led training. Learn advanced cardiac life support, airway management, ACLS pharmacology, and team-based resuscitation. Earn your AHA ACLS Provider eCard valid for two years.

courses.original_course_name: AHA - ACLS Provider Renewal - In-Person
courses.clean_title: AHA ACLS Provider – In-Person Renewal
courses.lander_title: AHA ACLS Renewal – In-Person Certification
courses.original_html:
AHA - ACLS Provider – In-person – Renewal

This ACLS Renewal course is for experienced healthcare professionals who currently hold an active or recently expired ACLS certification. This instructor-led, scenario-based course helps reinforce and update your skills in managing cardiovascular emergencies.

Course content includes advanced clinical interventions, team dynamics, airway management, rhythm recognition, pharmacology, and post-cardiac arrest care. This format is ideal for those working in emergency departments, ICUs, and other high-acuity settings.

Course Topics Include:

  • ACLS algorithms and post-cardiac arrest care
  • Airway management and rhythm recognition
  • High-quality CPR and AED use
  • Team-based response and effective communication
  • Acute coronary syndromes and stroke management
  • Clinical decision-making in life-threatening scenarios
courses.lander_html:
AHA ACLS Renewal – In-Person

The American Heart Association ACLS Renewal course is designed for experienced healthcare professionals who already hold ACLS certification and need to renew before expiration.

This instructor-led course reinforces advanced resuscitation skills using current AHA guidelines. Students review critical ACLS algorithms, airway management techniques, pharmacology, and team-based resuscitation strategies used during cardiopulmonary emergencies.

Who This Course Is For

  • Emergency department nurses
  • ICU and critical care staff
  • Paramedics and advanced EMTs
  • Physicians and physician assistants
  • Respiratory therapists
  • Healthcare providers responsible for advanced cardiac care

Course Length

Approximately 6–8 hours depending on class size and participant experience.

Certification

Students who successfully complete the course receive an American Heart Association ACLS Provider eCard valid for 2 years.
courses.seo_description: Renew your AHA ACLS certification with instructor-led training focused on cardiac arrest management, ACLS algorithms, airway skills, and team-based resuscitation. Earn your AHA ACLS Provider eCard valid for two years.

courses.original_course_name: AHA - ACLS Provider - Online or Skills Session
courses.clean_title: AHA ACLS Provider – HeartCode + Skills Session
courses.lander_title: AHA HeartCode ACLS – Online Course with Skills Session
courses.original_html:
AHA - ACLS Provider – HeartCode® Skills Session

The ACLS HeartCode® Skills Session is the hands-on component of the American Heart Association (AHA) ACLS certification. This session is for healthcare providers who have already completed the required online HeartCode® ACLS course.

During this in-person session led by an authorized AHA Instructor, you will demonstrate your ability to apply ACLS protocols, perform high-quality CPR, and manage simulated cardiovascular emergencies.
courses.lander_html:
AHA HeartCode ACLS – Online Course + Skills Session

The American Heart Association HeartCode ACLS course provides a flexible blended learning option for healthcare professionals who need Advanced Cardiac Life Support certification.

Students complete the interactive online HeartCode ACLS course at their own pace and then attend a short in-person skills session with an AHA instructor to demonstrate hands-on resuscitation skills.

How the HeartCode ACLS Course Works

  • Complete the HeartCode ACLS online course
  • Schedule and attend a short in-person skills session
  • Receive your American Heart Association ACLS Provider eCard

Course Format

This blended course combines online learning with an instructor-led skills evaluation. The in-person session typically lasts between 45 and 90 minutes.

Certification

Upon successful completion of the skills session, students receive the American Heart Association ACLS Provider eCard, valid for 2 years.
courses.seo_description: Complete AHA HeartCode ACLS online and finish with a short in-person skills session. Flexible certification for healthcare professionals. Earn your AHA ACLS Provider eCard valid for two years.

courses.original_course_name: AHA - PALS Provider - In Person - Renewal
courses.clean_title: AHA PALS Provider – In-Person Renewal
courses.lander_title: AHA PALS Renewal – In-Person Certification
courses.original_html:
AHA - PALS Provider – In-Person – Renewal

The American Heart Association (AHA) Pediatric Advanced Life Support (PALS) Provider Renewal Course is designed for healthcare professionals who currently hold a current or recently expired AHA PALS Provider card. This in-person, instructor-led course reinforces core pediatric emergency care skills through scenario-based instruction and hands-on practice.

Participants will refine pediatric assessment strategies, strengthen clinical decision-making skills, and demonstrate team-based resuscitation techniques using the AHA PALS algorithm. This course is ideal for providers working in emergency departments, intensive care, or other high-acuity environments.
courses.lander_html:
AHA PALS Renewal – In-Person

The American Heart Association Pediatric Advanced Life Support (PALS) Renewal course is designed for healthcare professionals who currently hold a current or recently expired AHA PALS Provider card and need renewal training.

This instructor-led classroom course reinforces pediatric emergency care skills through scenario-based instruction, hands-on practice, and current AHA PALS algorithms.

Who This Course Is For

  • Pediatric nurses
  • Emergency department staff
  • ICU and critical care clinicians
  • Paramedics
  • Respiratory therapists
  • Physicians and advanced practice providers

Course Length

Approximately 6–8 hours depending on class size and participant experience.

Certification

Upon successful completion, students receive the American Heart Association PALS Provider eCard, valid for 2 years.
courses.seo_description: Renew your AHA PALS certification with instructor-led pediatric advanced life support training. Review pediatric assessment, respiratory emergencies, shock, cardiac arrest, and team-based resuscitation. Earn your AHA PALS Provider eCard valid for two years.

courses.original_course_name: AHA - PALS Provider - Online or Skills Session - Initial or Renewal
courses.clean_title: AHA PALS Provider – HeartCode + Skills Session
courses.lander_title: AHA HeartCode PALS – Online Course with Skills Session
courses.original_html:
AHA - PALS Provider – HeartCode® Skills Session
Complete your AHA PALS certification with a self-paced online course and an in-person skills session.

Course fee includes hands-on skills testing and a 2-year AHA PALS eCard.
courses.lander_html:
AHA HeartCode PALS – Online Course + Skills Session

The American Heart Association HeartCode PALS course provides a flexible blended learning option for healthcare professionals who need Pediatric Advanced Life Support certification.

Students complete the interactive HeartCode PALS online course at their own pace and then attend a short in-person skills session with an AHA instructor to demonstrate hands-on pediatric resuscitation skills.

How the HeartCode PALS Course Works

  • Complete the HeartCode PALS online course
  • Schedule and attend a short in-person skills session
  • Demonstrate pediatric emergency response skills
  • Receive your American Heart Association PALS Provider eCard

Course Format

The in-person skills session typically lasts 45–90 minutes and includes hands-on practice and evaluation using pediatric resuscitation scenarios.

Certification

Upon successful completion of the skills session, students receive the American Heart Association PALS Provider eCard, valid for 2 years.
courses.seo_description: Complete AHA HeartCode PALS online and finish with a short in-person skills session. Flexible pediatric advanced life support certification for healthcare professionals. Earn your AHA PALS Provider eCard valid for two years.

courses.original_course_name: AHA - Heartsaver® CPR AED - In-person
courses.clean_title: AHA Heartsaver CPR AED – In-Person
courses.lander_title: CPR AED Certification – AHA Heartsaver CPR AED Class
courses.original_html:
This class is for any individual who is looking for self-improvement, basic CPR for work that does not require a First Aid component (although, if desired, First Aid can be added by registering HERE), or families looking to ensure safety in the house and around the pool area.
What is covered
  • Adult, child and infant CPR
  • Foreign-body airway obstruction
  • Automated External Defibrillator (AED)
Plan on spending <2 hours of interactive instruction and learning
Price Includes:
  • Education and interactive skills instruction adult, child, and infant CPR with AED
  • American Heart Association Course completion card for Heartsaver CPR for 2 years. 
***THIS COURSE IS NOT FOR HEALTHCARE PROVIDERS PROVIDING PATIENT CARE -->  Visit HERE for BLS options.
courses.lander_html:
CPR AED Certification – AHA Heartsaver CPR AED

The American Heart Association Heartsaver CPR AED course teaches individuals how to respond confidently to cardiac and breathing emergencies. This instructor-led class is designed for non-medical professionals who need CPR certification for work, school, or personal preparedness.

Students learn essential life-saving skills including adult, child, and infant CPR, how to use an Automated External Defibrillator (AED), and how to respond to choking emergencies.

Who This Course Is For

  • Teachers and school staff
  • Fitness instructors and coaches
  • Childcare providers and babysitters
  • Construction and workplace safety teams
  • Lifeguards and camp counselors
  • Parents and family members
  • Anyone who wants CPR training

Course Length

Approximately 2 hours.

Certification

Upon successful completion, students receive the American Heart Association Heartsaver CPR AED course completion card valid for 2 years.
courses.seo_description: Get CPR certified with the American Heart Association Heartsaver CPR AED course. Learn adult, child, and infant CPR, AED use, and choking response. Hands-on instructor-led training with a 2-year certification.

courses.original_course_name: AHA - Heartsaver® First Aid / CPR / AED Online + In-Person Skills
courses.clean_title: AHA Heartsaver First Aid CPR AED – Online + Skills Session
courses.lander_title: First Aid CPR AED Certification – AHA Heartsaver (Online + Skills)
courses.original_html:
The Dates/Times below are for a Hands-On Skills Session (Step 2) for this AHA Online Course.

* If you still need Step 1: After selecting a date, you will be able to purchase/select the required online portion.

The AHA Heartsaver® First Aid / CPR / AED (Online + In-Person Skills) course teaches participants to provide first aid, perform high-quality CPR, and use an automated external defibrillator (AED) safely and effectively.
courses.lander_html:
First Aid CPR AED Certification – AHA Heartsaver

The American Heart Association Heartsaver First Aid CPR AED course teaches individuals how to recognize and respond to common medical emergencies, injuries, and life-threatening situations.

This blended learning course combines self-paced online learning with a short in-person skills session led by an AHA instructor.

Blended Learning Format

Step 1 – Online Coursework

Students complete the self-paced Heartsaver First Aid CPR AED online course before attending the skills session. Estimated time: approximately 3 hours.

Step 2 – In-Person Skills Session

Students attend a hands-on session with an instructor to practice and demonstrate CPR, AED use, and first aid skills. Estimated time: approximately 2 hours.

Certification

After completing both parts, students receive the American Heart Association Heartsaver First Aid CPR AED eCard valid for 2 years.
courses.seo_description: Get certified in First Aid, CPR, and AED with the American Heart Association Heartsaver course. Complete online training and finish with a hands-on skills session. OSHA-compliant certification valid for 2 years.

courses.original_course_name: AHA - Heartsaver® Pediatric First Aid / CPR / AED In-person
courses.clean_title: AHA Heartsaver Pediatric First Aid CPR AED – In-Person
courses.lander_title: Childcare CPR & First Aid Certification (AHA Pediatric CPR AED)
courses.course_type: Heartsaver Pediatric First Aid CPR AED
courses.original_html:
AHA - Heartsaver® Pediatric First Aid / CPR / AED – In-person

The American Heart Association (AHA) Heartsaver® Pediatric First Aid CPR AED course is designed for anyone involved in childcare who needs a certification for work or regulatory purposes, including daycare staff, camp counselors, teachers, coaches, foster parents, and others responsible for children’s safety.

This instructor-led, classroom-based training provides participants with the knowledge and hands-on skills to respond to pediatric medical emergencies, injuries, and cardiac arrest.
courses.lander_html:
Childcare CPR & First Aid Certification (AHA Pediatric CPR AED)

The American Heart Association Heartsaver® Pediatric First Aid CPR AED course is designed for people responsible for the safety of infants and children. This instructor-led class teaches participants how to recognize and respond to pediatric medical emergencies, injuries, and cardiac arrest.

This course is commonly required for childcare workers, daycare staff, teachers, coaches, camp counselors, and foster parents who must maintain CPR and First Aid certification for employment or regulatory compliance.

Who This Course Is For

  • Daycare and preschool staff
  • Childcare providers and babysitters
  • Teachers and school staff
  • Camp counselors and youth program leaders
  • Foster parents and guardians
  • Coaches and youth sports staff

Certification

Students who successfully complete the course receive a 2-year American Heart Association Heartsaver Pediatric First Aid CPR AED eCard.
courses.seo_description: Childcare CPR & First Aid certification with AHA Heartsaver Pediatric First Aid CPR AED training. Ideal for daycare staff, teachers, coaches, and childcare providers. Includes hands-on training and a 2-year AHA certification card.

courses.original_course_name: ARC - BLS - In-Person Classroom
courses.clean_title: American Red Cross BLS Provider – In-Person
courses.lander_title: Red Cross BLS Certification – In-Person Class
courses.original_html:
American Red Cross - Basic Life Support (BLS)

The American Red Cross Basic Life Support (BLS) Provider course is designed for healthcare professionals and first responders who need to develop or refresh their life-saving skills in a structured, hands-on environment.
courses.lander_html:
Red Cross BLS Certification – In-Person Class

The American Red Cross Basic Life Support (BLS) Provider course is designed for healthcare professionals and first responders who need hands-on BLS training in a classroom setting.

This instructor-led course focuses on high-performance CPR, AED use, airway management, ventilation, and team-based response in adult, child, and infant emergencies.

Certification

Students who successfully complete the course receive a Digital American Red Cross BLS Provider certificate valid for 2 years.
courses.seo_description: Get Red Cross BLS certified with hands-on instructor-led training for healthcare professionals and first responders. Learn high-quality CPR, AED use, airway management, and team-based resuscitation. Earn your Red Cross BLS certificate valid for two years.

courses.original_course_name: ARC - BLS - Blended Learning Online - Initial or Renewal
courses.clean_title: American Red Cross BLS Provider – Blended Learning
courses.lander_title: Red Cross BLS Certification – Online Course with Skills Check
courses.original_html:
ARC - Basic Life Support (Blended Learning | Skills Session)
Complete your ARC BLS certification with online coursework and an in-person skills check.

Train on your schedule with American Red Cross Blended Learning. This ARC Basic Life Support (BLS) Course combines self-paced online learning with a hands-on skills check led by an ARC-certified instructor.
courses.lander_html:
Red Cross BLS Certification – Online Course with Skills Check

The American Red Cross Basic Life Support (BLS) blended learning course gives students a flexible way to complete BLS certification by combining online coursework with an in-person skills check.

This format is ideal for healthcare professionals, students, and staff who need Red Cross BLS certification but prefer to complete the knowledge portion on their own schedule before attending a hands-on evaluation.

How the Course Works

  • Complete the American Red Cross BLS online course and test
  • Schedule and attend an in-person ARC BLS skills check
  • Present your online completion certificate at the session
  • Receive your Red Cross BLS digital certificate after successful completion
courses.seo_description: Complete Red Cross BLS certification with flexible online learning and an in-person skills check. Ideal for healthcare professionals, students, and staff who need ARC BLS certification with a 2-year digital certificate.

courses.original_course_name: ARC BLS Challenge - Renewal for experienced Providers
courses.clean_title: ARC BLS Challenge – Skills Assessment Renewal
courses.lander_title: Red Cross BLS Challenge (Assessment-Only Renewal)
courses.original_html:
🚨 BLS Challenge – Assessment-Only Option for Experienced Providers

This is an assessment-only session—an American Red Cross BLS Challenge—designed for experienced providers who need to renew their certification. There is no instruction or remediation during this session.

🔥 IMPORTANT:

  • You must demonstrate all BLS skills and pass a written test to earn certification.
  • This is not a training course. You must already know the skills like the back of your hand.
  • If you fail the Challenge, you will not be certified and must complete the full BLS course at a later date.
courses.lander_html:
Red Cross BLS Challenge – Assessment-Only Renewal

The American Red Cross BLS Challenge is an assessment-only option designed for experienced healthcare providers who already know the BLS skills and simply need to renew their certification.

This session is not a traditional class. There is no instruction or remediation. Participants must demonstrate the required skills and pass the written assessment to earn certification.

Important Requirements

  • You must successfully demonstrate all required BLS skills
  • You must pass the Red Cross BLS written exam
  • This is not a training class
  • No coaching or remediation is provided during the challenge
courses.seo_description: Fast Red Cross BLS renewal for experienced providers. The ARC BLS Challenge is an assessment-only option with skills testing and written exam for confident healthcare professionals needing BLS recertification.

courses.original_course_name: Elementary First Aid ONLINE to meet U.S. Coast Guard Maritime Elementary First Aid and CPR Requirement
courses.clean_title: USCG Elementary First Aid & CPR – Online + Skills
courses.lander_title: USCG Captain License First Aid & CPR Certification
courses.original_html:
** Same-day certification — AHA Heartsaver® eCard + AMERHA-216 **
Receive the SAME certification card as the in-person class
Complete the coursework online at your own pace
Then select your preferred date below for the required in-person skills check

This blended learning option allows you to complete the American Heart Association’s Heartsaver® First Aid CPR AED course online, followed by a brief hands-on skills session with a certified instructor.
courses.lander_html:
USCG Captain License First Aid & CPR Certification

This course provides the training required to meet the U.S. Coast Guard Elementary First Aid and CPR requirement for Merchant Mariner Credential (MMC) applicants and Captain License holders.

The training is based on the American Heart Association Heartsaver® First Aid CPR AED program and includes the documentation required for U.S. Coast Guard compliance.

Certification Issued

  • AHA Heartsaver First Aid CPR AED eCard (valid for 2 years)
  • AMERHA-216 documentation accepted by the U.S. Coast Guard
courses.seo_description: USCG Captain License First Aid and CPR certification. Includes AHA Heartsaver First Aid CPR AED training plus AMERHA-216 documentation accepted for Merchant Mariner Credential (MMC) and Coast Guard requirements.

courses.original_course_name: HSI BLS and Adult First Aid | Blended Learning
courses.clean_title: HSI BLS + Adult First Aid – Blended Learning
courses.lander_title: BLS + First Aid Certification (HSI Blended Learning)
courses.original_html:
HSI BLS and Adult First Aid – Blended Learning

This HSI blended learning course combines Basic Life Support (BLS) and Adult First Aid/CPR/AED into a single certification.
courses.lander_html:
BLS + First Aid Certification (HSI Blended Learning)

This course combines Basic Life Support (BLS) and Adult First Aid / CPR / AED into a single certification program.

The blended learning format allows students to complete the training online and then attend a short in-person session to demonstrate the required hands-on skills.

Compliance and Workplace Requirements

This course is commonly used to meet requirements for Trillium behavioral health provider requirements, TRICARE BLS requirements for ABA providers, and insurance and licensing standards requiring BLS and First Aid.

Certification

Upon successful completion, students receive a 2-year HSI certification covering both Basic Life Support and Adult First Aid.
courses.seo_description: BLS and First Aid certification in one course. HSI blended learning program with online training and in-person skills testing. Ideal for ABA providers, RBTs, healthcare staff, and workplaces needing both certifications.

courses.original_course_name: AHA - BLS - Become an American Heart Association Instructor
courses.clean_title: AHA BLS Instructor Course
courses.lander_title: Become an American Heart Association BLS Instructor
courses.original_html:
🚨 Become a BLS Instructor

Want to teach CPR professionally — without the stress of sounding like a robot? You're in the right place.

This course is for anyone looking to certify others in BLS and Heartsaver-level CPR/First Aid, whether you're teaching staff at your job, launching your own gig, or just want to expand your skills.

✅ What’s Included

Your registration includes:

  • BLS Instructor Essentials (online course)
  • BLS Instructor Manual (eBook or print, shipped to you)
  • BLS Provider Manual (for reference and student prep)
  • In-person Instructor Course
  • Initial Instructor Monitoring (within 6 months)
  • Training site alignment (with us, if needed)
courses.lander_html:
Become an American Heart Association BLS Instructor

This course prepares qualified candidates to become certified American Heart Association Basic Life Support (BLS) Instructors.

Once certified, instructors can teach AHA BLS Provider courses and Heartsaver CPR and First Aid classes through an authorized training site.

What’s Included

  • AHA BLS Instructor Essentials online course
  • AHA BLS Instructor Manual
  • AHA BLS Provider Manual
  • In-person instructor training session
  • Required instructor monitoring session
  • Optional alignment with our AHA training site
courses.seo_description: Become an American Heart Association BLS Instructor. Includes Instructor Essentials, instructor course, teaching evaluation, and certification to teach BLS and Heartsaver CPR courses nationwide.

courses.original_course_name: AHA - Family & Friends® CPR
courses.clean_title: AHA Family & Friends CPR
courses.lander_title: CPR Class for Parents & Families (Family & Friends CPR)
courses.original_html:
The Family & Friends CPR course is intended for anyone who wants to learn CPR and prefers to learn in 
an Instructor-led or facilitated group environment. This course is for people who do not need a course completion card for a job or other requirements.
The course is ideal for parents, grandparents, caregivers, school groups and students, and others interested in learning how to save a life.
courses.lander_html:
CPR Class for Parents, Families & Caregivers

The American Heart Association Family & Friends® CPR course is a relaxed, hands-on class designed for people who want to learn lifesaving CPR skills without the pressure of testing or certification.

This course is perfect for families, caregivers, and community groups who want to be prepared to respond to emergencies involving adults, children, or infants.

Certification

Family & Friends CPR is not a certification course.
courses.seo_description: CPR class for parents, grandparents, babysitters, and caregivers. Learn adult, child, and infant CPR and choking relief in a relaxed, no-test Family & Friends CPR class. Perfect for families and community groups.

courses.original_course_name: AHA - Heartsaver® First Aid / CPR / AED In-person
courses.clean_title: AHA Heartsaver First Aid CPR AED – In-Person
courses.lander_title: First Aid CPR AED Certification – AHA Heartsaver In-Person Class
courses.original_html:
The Heartsaver First Aid / CPR / AED program covers Adult, Child, Infant CPR with AED and Choking with various First Aid topics.
Designed for employee training in workplaces, scouting leaders, coaches, and non-medical home care workers. For employees and/or designated first responders who have a duty to respond to a first aid or cardiac emergency because of job responsibilities or regulatory requirements.
What is covered
  • Adult first aid (first aid basics, medical and injury emergencies)
  • Adult, child and infant CPR
  • Foreign-body airway obstruction
  • Use of an Automated External Defibrillator (AED)
Includes 2-year AHA Completion Card
courses.lander_html:
First Aid CPR AED Certification – AHA Heartsaver In-Person Class

The American Heart Association Heartsaver First Aid CPR AED course is an instructor-led classroom program for people who need first aid and CPR certification for work, regulatory compliance, or general preparedness.

This class teaches participants how to respond to medical emergencies, injury emergencies, choking, and sudden cardiac arrest using adult, child, and infant CPR and AED skills.

Who This Course Is For

  • Workplace safety responders
  • Teachers and school staff
  • Coaches and scout leaders
  • Construction and industrial employees
  • Non-medical home care workers
  • Anyone required to have OSHA-aligned first aid and CPR training

What You Will Learn

  • First aid basics and scene assessment
  • Medical emergencies
  • Injury emergencies
  • Adult, child, and infant CPR
  • Relief of choking
  • Use of an Automated External Defibrillator (AED)

Course Format

This is a traditional instructor-led classroom course with hands-on skills practice and testing.

Students complete all training in one visit, making this a practical option for people who want a straightforward in-person certification experience.

Certification

Students who successfully complete the course receive a 2-year American Heart Association Heartsaver First Aid CPR AED completion card.

Workplace Compliance

This course is commonly used for workplace responders and aligns with OSHA expectations for employees who may need to respond to first aid or cardiac emergencies as part of their job duties.

Why This Course Matters

For many students, this is the simplest path: one class, one visit, one certification card covering first aid, CPR, and AED.
courses.seo_description: Instructor-led AHA Heartsaver First Aid CPR AED training. Hands-on classroom course for workplace responders, teachers, coaches, and non-medical staff. Learn first aid, CPR, choking relief, and AED use with a 2-year certification card.

courses.original_course_name: AHA - Heartsaver® Pediatric First Aid / CPR / AED Online + In-Person Skills
courses.clean_title: AHA Heartsaver Pediatric First Aid CPR AED – Online + Skills Session
courses.lander_title: Pediatric First Aid CPR AED Certification – AHA Heartsaver (Online + Skills)
courses.course_type: Heartsaver Pediatric First Aid CPR AED (Online + Skills)
courses.original_html:
🧸 Heartsaver Pediatric First Aid CPR AED

Be ready. Be confident. Be the one who helps.

Learn to recognize and respond to the most common childhood emergencies with the American Heart Association’s Heartsaver® Pediatric First Aid CPR AED course — a must-have for anyone responsible for the safety of infants and children.

  • 🖥️ Part 1: Complete the interactive online modules at your own pace
  • 🏥 Part 2: Attend a brief, hands-on skills check to complete certification
  • ✅ AHA certification valid for 2 years
  • 💵 All included in one simple price!

📚 What You’ll Learn:

  • 🤒 Illness & injury response
  • 🩹 Bleeding control & bandaging
  • 🤧 Allergic reactions & EpiPen use
  • 😮‍💨 Asthma emergencies
  • 🌊 Drowning response
  • 🐝 Bites & stings
  • 🔥 Burns
  • 💓 CPR & AED for children and infants
  • 🫁 Choking relief techniques
  • 👨‍🦳 (Optional) Adult CPR & AED overview
courses.lander_html:
Pediatric First Aid CPR AED Certification – AHA Heartsaver (Online + Skills)

The American Heart Association Heartsaver Pediatric First Aid CPR AED course prepares caregivers, teachers, and childcare professionals to respond confidently to emergencies involving infants and children.

This blended learning format allows students to complete the knowledge portion online at their own pace, followed by a short in-person skills session to demonstrate hands-on CPR and first aid techniques.

Who This Course Is For

  • Childcare providers and daycare staff
  • Teachers and school employees
  • Summer camp staff
  • Youth sports coaches
  • Foster parents and guardians
  • Babysitters and nannies
  • Parents and grandparents

What You Will Learn

  • Pediatric illness recognition and response
  • Bleeding control and injury care
  • Allergic reactions and epinephrine use
  • Asthma emergencies
  • Bites, stings, and burns
  • Drowning response
  • Infant and child CPR
  • Use of an Automated External Defibrillator (AED)
  • Relief of choking

Course Format

This course uses the American Heart Association blended learning format.

  • Part 1: Online self-paced training covering pediatric first aid and CPR concepts
  • Part 2: In-person skills session with an instructor

The hands-on session focuses on practicing CPR, AED use, and choking relief skills for infants and children.

Certification

Students who successfully complete both parts receive an American Heart Association Heartsaver Pediatric First Aid CPR AED completion card valid for two years.

Regulatory Use

This course is commonly used to meet childcare provider CPR and first aid training requirements in many states, including programs regulated under childcare licensing agencies.
courses.seo_description: AHA Heartsaver Pediatric First Aid CPR AED certification for childcare providers, teachers, and caregivers. Blended learning format with online training and hands-on skills session. Certification valid for two years.

courses.original_course_name: HSI Adult First Aid | Adult/Child/Infant CPR AED
courses.clean_title: HSI Adult First Aid CPR AED – In-Person
courses.lander_title: First Aid CPR AED Certification – HSI In-Person Course
courses.course_type: HSI Adult First Aid CPR AED
courses.original_html:
HSI Adult First Aid | CPR AED - In-Person Training

The HSI Adult First Aid | CPR AED course is a nationally recognized, instructor-led program that teaches essential skills for responding to medical emergencies involving adults, children, and infants.

📌 Course Highlights:

  • Comprehensive Curriculum: Includes CPR, AED use, choking relief, bleeding control, burns, shock, and more.
  • Evidence-Based Training: Follows the latest ILCOR resuscitation science and the 2020 AHA Guidelines for CPR and ECC.
courses.lander_html:
First Aid CPR AED Certification – HSI In-Person Course

The HSI Adult First Aid CPR AED course provides practical, instructor-led training for individuals who may need to respond to medical emergencies in the workplace, community, or home.

Participants learn how to recognize life-threatening emergencies, provide first aid, perform CPR for adults, children, and infants, and safely use an automated external defibrillator (AED).

Who This Course Is For

  • Workplace safety teams
  • Teachers and school staff
  • Coaches and athletic staff
  • Fitness instructors
  • Residential caregivers
  • Community members who want practical emergency skills

What You Will Learn

  • First aid basics and scene safety
  • Bleeding control and injury response
  • Burns and shock
  • Medical emergencies
  • Adult, child, and infant CPR
  • Relief of choking
  • Use of an Automated External Defibrillator (AED)

Course Format

This is a traditional instructor-led classroom course that includes demonstration, guided practice, and hands-on skills evaluation with a certified instructor.

Certification

Students who successfully complete the course receive an HSI / ASHI CPR AED and Basic First Aid certification card valid for two years.

Workplace Compliance

This training meets OSHA expectations for workplace first aid responders under 29 CFR 1910.151.

Maritime Credential Option

Students pursuing a Merchant Mariner Credential may also receive documentation meeting the U.S. Coast Guard Elementary First Aid and CPR requirement.
courses.seo_description: HSI Adult First Aid CPR AED certification class. Instructor-led training covering CPR, AED use, choking relief, and emergency first aid for adults, children, and infants. Meets OSHA workplace training expectations.

courses.original_course_name: HSI BLS | Blended Learning
courses.clean_title: HSI Basic Life Support (BLS) – Blended Learning
courses.lander_title: HSI BLS Certification – Online Course with In-Person Skills Session
courses.lander_html:
HSI BLS Certification – Online Course with In-Person Skills Session

The HSI Basic Life Support (BLS) course prepares participants to recognize life-threatening emergencies and provide high-quality CPR using current resuscitation science and international treatment recommendations.

This blended learning format allows students to complete the knowledge portion online followed by an in-person skills session with an instructor.

Who This Course Is For

  • Healthcare workers whose employer accepts HSI BLS certification
  • Dental offices and outpatient clinics
  • Emergency response teams
  • Professional rescuers
  • Anyone needing professional-level CPR training

What You Will Learn

  • High-quality CPR for adults, children, and infants
  • Use of an Automated External Defibrillator (AED)
  • Team-based resuscitation principles
  • Relief of choking
  • Scene safety and patient assessment

Course Format

This course uses a blended learning format:

  • Part 1: Online self-paced training modules
  • Part 2: In-person hands-on skills evaluation with an instructor

Certification

Students who successfully complete both parts receive an HSI Basic Life Support certification card valid for two years.

Choosing the Right BLS Course

Many hospitals and healthcare employers require the American Heart Association (AHA) BLS certification. Students should confirm employer requirements before enrolling in an HSI BLS course.
courses.seo_description: HSI Basic Life Support (BLS) certification course with online learning and in-person skills testing. Covers CPR, AED use, choking relief, and team resuscitation skills. Certification valid for two years.

courses.original_course_name: STOP THE BLEED®
courses.clean_title: STOP THE BLEED® – Bleeding Control Training
courses.lander_title: STOP THE BLEED® Training – Learn to Control Life-Threatening Bleeding
courses.cert_body: STOP THE BLEED® / American College of Surgeons
courses.original_html:
STOP THE BLEED® Interactive Course

Stop The Bleed is a national campaign that aims to make bleeding control training as commonplace as CPR training.

This interactive course is a virtual alternative to the in-person lecture presentation. The STOP THE BLEED® Interactive Course guides individuals through the three methods of bleeding control using video demonstrations, interactive learning, and spontaneous quizzes.

910CPR believes strongly that this information is valuable and want to bring this free program to your attention.

Although the information above is free, to recieve a STOP THE BLEED® certification, there is a nominal fee to cover materials and manikin wear.
courses.lander_html:
STOP THE BLEED® Training – Learn to Control Life-Threatening Bleeding

Severe bleeding is the number one preventable cause of death after traumatic injury. The STOP THE BLEED® program teaches ordinary people how to recognize life-threatening bleeding and take immediate action until professional help arrives.

In many emergencies, the person who saves a life is not a doctor or paramedic. It is someone already on scene. Until help arrives, you are the help.

What You Will Learn

  • How to recognize life-threatening bleeding
  • How to apply direct pressure
  • How to pack a wound
  • How to properly apply a tourniquet
  • How to respond safely in traumatic emergencies

Course Options

Participants may complete the STOP THE BLEED® interactive online training for free.

For those who want hands-on experience, a short in-person skills session is available to practice bleeding control techniques using training equipment.

Who Should Take This Training

  • Teachers and school staff
  • Construction and industrial workers
  • Security personnel
  • Church safety teams
  • Coaches and athletic staff
  • Parents and community members
courses.seo_description: STOP THE BLEED® training teaches how to control life-threatening bleeding using pressure, wound packing, and tourniquets. Learn essential emergency response skills and be prepared to act before first responders arrive.
